utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. Now check each in the final coverage plan: (1) health_coverage: present with physical and mental health and costs; includes dependent coverage and a total—meets requirement of 'physical and mental health insurance with costs'. Fully specified. (2) property_coverage: present, but only 'home_contents' with a cost; the required format asks for 'home/contents insurance with costs'—the home/building part is missing, so not fully specified. (3) travel_coverage: present with base plan, add-ons, and total, all with costs—fully specified. (4) additional_services: present with items and costs—fully specified as 'any enhancement services with costs'. (5) total_budget: present with complete monthly cost breakdown—fully specified. Therefore, 4 items are fully specified out of 5.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: I matched each named item in the final coverage plan to the closest exact item name available in the ground-truth ratings. For health physical, the plan specifies 'EliteCare Premium Plan' which exactly exists under primary_health_coverage.physical_health. For mental health, the plan specifies 'PrimeCare Premium Mental Health Plan' which corresponds to 'PrimeCare Premium Plan' under primary_health_coverage.mental_health. For property, 'PrimeHome Premium Coverage' exactly matches property_protection.home_content. For travel, the final plan uses generic names; the closest premium 5-star options in the ratings are 'PrimeTravel Premium Plan' or 'EliteTravel Premium Plan'. Since no exact name is provided, I map the base plan to the better-matching premium option from the ratings; either PrimeTravel or EliteTravel would be valid with rating 10, but I select 'PrimeTravel Premium Plan' to represent a premium executive plan. The add-ons described as 'Premium evacuation & targeted pre-existing coverage' align with '24/7 Travel Assistance' and 'Premium Medical Network' in travel_enhancement_services; I map them to both relevant rated services. The 'Concierge Medical Services' aligns most closely with 'Specialist Access' among health enhancement services. There is no exact item for 'Unified family package for adult children' or service fees/buffers, so they are not mapped. 'Integrated company benefits' is not in ratings. Thus, only items with clear counterparts are mapped.
